data.title: Ferrellgas Partners, L.P. reported financial results for Q1 FY 2026. Margin per gallon increased 6%, revenue decreased by 2%, and Adjusted EBITDA decreased by 18% to $29.3 million. Net loss was $26.9 million in Q1 FY 2026. Operational highlights include increased sales and improvements in customer retention and new customer acquisition. Ferrellgas conducted a teleconference on Dec. 12, 2025, to discuss the results.
data.description: Ferrellgas Partners, L.P. reported its financial results for the first fiscal quarter ending October 31, 2025. The company redeemed $650.0 million of Senior Notes due 2026, issued $650.0 million of new Senior Notes due 2031, and expanded their revolving credit facility. Margin per gallon increased by 6%, with a 2% decrease in revenue offset by a 5% decrease in product costs. Average propane prices dropped by 2.9%. Adjusted EBITDA decreased by 18% to $29.3 million. The company reported a net loss of $26.9 million. Operational highlights included improvements in sales, customer service, and route optimization. The company emphasized customer retention and strategic growth opportunities. Ferrellgas conducted a teleconference to discuss its financial results and future plans. Ferrellgas serves propane customers nationally, with its Blue Rhino brand available at over 64,000 locations.

data.title: Weekly recap: 13 tech press releases including IBM's acquisition of Confluent and AliExpress' counterfeit bust.
data.description: This week in tech news, IBM made headlines by acquiring Confluent, while NextEra Energy and Google Cloud formed a partnership in the energy sector. Additionally, AliExpress conducted a successful counterfeit bust. The announcements were made on Dec. 12, 2025,as detailed in PR Newswire. The tech world was abuzz with IBM's acquisition of Confluent, a deal worth a substantial sum. NextEra Energy and Google Cloud also made waves with their energy partnership, aimed at revolutionizing the industry. AliExpress garnered attention for cracking down on counterfeit products, a move that highlighted the ongoing battle against fraudulent goods. The week was marked by these significant developments, underscoring the dynamism and innovation in the tech sector.

data.title: 123M people globally displaced by end of 2024, highest ever recorded, stressing need for clean water to prevent disease.
data.description: In Billinghurst, England, as of December 12, 2025, over 123 million individuals globally have been compelled to leave their homes, marking a record high as reported by the UN Refugee Agency (UNHCR). This alarming number signifies the immense scale of displacement seen by the end of 2024. The data indicates a significant rise in the number of forcibly displaced people, emphasizing the urgent need for humanitarian assistance. Among these, many individuals are currently residing in refugee camps, highlighting the ongoing challenges faced by millions worldwide.

data.title: Shoe Carnival announces $0.15 quarterly dividend and $50 million share repurchase program. Board of Directors approval.
data.description: In Fort Mill, S.C., Shoe Carnival, Inc. (Nasdaq: SCVL), a prominent retailer of footwear and accessories, disclosed that the Board of Directors has sanctioned a quarterly cash dividend of $0.15 per share. This dividend is scheduled to be disbursed on January 26, 2026, to shareholders holding shares as of January 12, 2026. Furthermore, the Board of Directors greenlit a new share repurchase scheme allowing for the buyback of up to $50 million worth of its common stock.

data.title: Rock Tech Lithium Inc. (TSX-V: RCK) has engaged ICP Securities Inc. for automated market making services.
data.description: On December 12, 2025, a lithium company based in Toronto, Rock Tech Lithium Inc., released news stating its engagement with ICP Securities Inc. for automated market making services. The company's stock symbols include TSX-V: RCK, OTCQX: RCKTF, FWB: RJIB, WKN: A1XF0V. The partnership will enhance market liquidity and facilitate trading for investors.

data.title: Executive Director of AngloGold Ashanti, Alberto Calderon, sells shares, retains 338,849 shares and 253,965 shares under vesting plan.
data.description: AngloGold Ashanti plc (NYSE: AU; JSE: ANG) has declared that one of its Executive Directors, Alberto Calderon, recently conducted a transaction involving the Company's securities. After the sale, Mr. Calderon retains ownership of 338,849 shares and share incentive awards. Additionally, he has 253,965 shares awaiting vesting under the Performance Share Plan. The transaction occurred on December 10, 2021.

data.title: Weekly roundup of health PR releases: highlights include increased demand in physical therapy & study on mindfulness in Millennials & Gen Z.
data.description: This week, a collection of significant healthcare announcements were highlighted by PR Newswire, with increasing interest observed in the field of physical therapy. A research study on the mindfulness practices of Millennials and Gen Z also caught attention. The press releases, released in New York on December 12, 2025, covered a wide array of healthcare topics. The demand for physical therapy services has been rising steadily, with a surge in patient appointments reaching record numbers. Additionally, the study focusing on mindfulness among Millennials and Gen Z has provided valuable insights into the mental well-being practices of the younger generation. This week's press releases offer a glimpse into the evolving landscape of healthcare, with numbers indicating growing trends and emerging areas of interest within the industry.

data.title: SEC Chair Paul Atkins eyes tokenized US financial markets with on-chain settlement after DTCC greenlights.
data.description: SEC Chair Paul Atkins reaffirmed his support for tokenized US financial markets and onchain settlement during comments on Thursday. Atkins emphasized the potential benefits of digitizing financial assets and streamlining settlement processes. This vision aligns with his commitment to embracing technological innovation in the financial sector.

data.title: Johnson Outdoors expected to report $0.68 loss per share on $117.24 million revenue before the bell on December 12, 2025.
data.description: Before the opening bell, Johnson Outdoors is expected to announce a quarterly loss of $0.68 per share with revenues reaching $117.24 million. In addition, Zedge will also be reporting their financial results. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.
